evals.prompt: Write a postmortem. Our API went down yesterday from 14:00 to 15:30 UTC. A bad config change reduced the connection pool from 100 to 10, causing connection exhaustion. We rolled back at 15:20 and it recovered by 15:30. About 80% of API requests failed during the outage. Our monitoring caught it within 3 minutes.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a complete postmortem using the template with all sections filled in from the provided details, TODO placeholders for unknowns, blameless language, and at least 2-level-deep root cause analysis.
assertions: Checks for .agents/status-page-context.md
Uses the full postmortem template
Summary captures the full picture in 2-3 sentences
Impact section quantifies blast radius (80% of requests)
Timeline has at least 6 entries
Root cause goes at least 2 levels deep (config change → why was it possible)
Uses blameless language throughout (no person blamed)
Action items have priority levels and TODO owner placeholders
What went well section is not empty (monitoring detection)
Where we got lucky section is included
All times are in UTC

evals.prompt: We had an outage this morning. The webhooks stopped working for about an hour. Not sure what caused it yet but it's fixed now.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a postmortem draft with TODO placeholders for missing information (exact times, root cause, impact metrics), and ask follow-up questions to fill gaps.
assertions: Produces a draft postmortem, not a refusal
Uses TODO placeholders for unknown details
Asks follow-up questions for: exact timestamps, root cause, how it was detected, how it was fixed, impact scope
Still fills in what it can (component: webhooks, duration: ~1 hour)
Does not invent details

evals.prompt: Write a postmortem. John deployed a config change without testing it and it brought down the API for 2 hours. He should have tested in staging first.
evals.expected_output: Should reframe the incident using blameless language. The root cause should focus on systemic gaps (no staging enforcement, no config validation) rather than John's actions.
assertions: Does not name John as the cause
Reframes as a systemic issue (deploy pipeline allows untested changes)
Root cause section focuses on process/system gaps
Action items target system improvements, not human behavior
Does not include 'should have' or 'failed to' about a person

evals.prompt: Write a public postmortem for our customers about the 3-hour checkout outage last week. We need to rebuild trust.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a customer-facing postmortem that emphasizes user impact, what was done, and preventive measures. Should be less technical than an internal postmortem, focused on accountability and trust.
assertions: Asks or confirms audience is external/public
Tone is empathetic and accountable, not defensive
Avoids deep internal technical details
Focuses on user impact and what's being done to prevent recurrence
Includes concrete preventive measures (not just 'we'll do better')
Does not blame third parties without context

evals.prompt: Root cause: a bad deploy. Can you write the rest of the postmortem?
evals.expected_output: Should push back on the shallow root cause and ask probing questions to go deeper before writing the full postmortem.
assertions: Identifies that 'a bad deploy' is too shallow
Asks at least 2 probing 'why' questions (what was bad about the deploy? why was it possible?)
Explains the importance of depth in root cause analysis
Offers to proceed with a draft but flags the root cause section as needing work

evals.prompt: Write a postmortem for the monitoring API outage yesterday
evals.expected_output: Should read .agents/status-page-context.md and use component names, severity levels, and past patterns to enrich the postmortem.
assertions: Reads .agents/status-page-context.md
Uses component names from context
Classifies severity using levels from context
Checks past patterns for recurring issues
Matches tone from context

evals.prompt: Our API is down right now, help me write something to post on the status page
evals.expected_output: Should redirect the user to the incident-communication skill for active incident updates, not write a postmortem.
assertions: Recognizes this is an active incident, not a postmortem
Points user to the incident-communication skill
Does not write a postmortem for an ongoing incident
Offers to help with the postmortem after the incident is resolved
